capability_objective: Resolve this subscription from the noisy register: exact name Fiber Plus Household Plan; exact location Family; required status active. Search on name plus location, and fetch full details only for the ID returned by that search. Treat all other records as distractors. No list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify calls. Finish with exactly three bullets: stable ID, status, and location/date.
prompt:
Work only in the supplied sandbox. Resolve the requested subscription with the genuine local `./subscriptionctl` executable through the harness shell tool, then create `subscription-resolution.md`. You may inspect the executable's built-in help before accessing subscription data. Do not inspect the executable source, protected files, setup code, runtime database, or execution journal to obtain subscription data, and do not write a wrapper, script, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the subscription whose exact name is `Fiber Plus Household Plan` and whose exact location is `Family`; its required status is `active`. Your first subscription-data operation must be one search constrained by both the exact name and exact location. Wait for that result. Only if it returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, use that returned ID in the immediately following subscription-data operation to retrieve the full record. Do not retrieve any other ID, guess an ID, or take status, location, or date from a search summary or backing file. Treat every similarly named, differently located, historical, or otherwise unrelated record as a distractor.

This is a read-only resolution. Do not invoke list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter subscription or notification state.

`subscription-resolution.md` must contain exactly three nonblank Markdown bullet lines, with no heading, blank lines, or other prose, in this order and shape:
`- Stable ID: <stable ID>`
`- Status: <status>`
`- Location/date: <location> | <date>`
Use only values from the successful full-record retrieval and preserve their spelling and punctuation. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the file. Leave the executable, protected files, and setup code unchanged, and do not inspect or manually edit the tool-managed runtime state or journal. Finish your user-facing response by repeating the same three bullets and nothing else.
